course_description:
Integrate and apply your knowledge and skills in pharmacy practice and medicines management. Develop your professional identity and approach to practice. Demonstrate professional decision-making skills to improve health outcomes employing knowledge of pharmacology, physiology, therapeutics and evidence-based practice. Demonstrate your skills in providing patient-centred care, medication review and dispensing medicines safely and effectively.

PHRM4102 is designed to develop and assess your readiness to become an Intern Pharmacist using the Australian Pharmacy Council (APC) Performance Outcomes. The knowledge and skills expected of a graduate are divided into five domains:

As such, all assessment items in the course contribute marks towards specific domains, and you will be required to accumulate at least 50% of the marks available in each domain over the semester to demonstrate proficiency.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
PHRM4102 is taught through UQ Extend and in-person Tutorials. The course is divided into three simultaneous streams:
Advanced medication management
Professional responsibilities
Extending pharmacy practice
The time commitment to this course is 20 hours per week. This is comprised of active online learning in UQ Extend for all three streams, attending three timetabled Tutorials (one for each stream), and working on assessments.
course_aims: Demonstrate essential scientific, clinical and professional knowledge in medicines management. Demonstrate professional decision-making and skills in collaborative care. Demonstrate skills in patient-centred care, dispensing and medication review in a wide variety and increasingly complex scenarios.

learning_outcomes.description: Justify professional decisions by appealing to relevant ethical and legal frameworks and take responsibility for your decisions.
Provide comprehensive, patient-centred medication management plans to ensure the safe, effective and judicious use of medicines.
Communicate effectively in a range of pharmacy practice contexts.
Improve use of medicines by intervening at a system level within a health care setting.
Provide patient-centred, socially and culturally appropriate care in a range of pharmacy practice contexts.
Assess ambulatory conditions and recommend appropriate management approaches, including pharmacological, non-pharmacological and referral options where appropriate.
Demonstrate teamwork behaviours as appropriate to the roles of pharmacists within health care teams, including oral and written communication.
Prescribe medications in accordance with current jurisdiction-specific legislation, scope of practice and professional guidance.
Reflect on your knowledge and capabilities as part of continuing professional development.

assessment_summary.due_date: 22/09/2025; This exam will occur during your Stream 2 Tutorial

assessment_details.title: Pre-course Reflection
assessment_details.task_description: Write a critical reflection on your perceived gaps in your knowledge and skills when comparing against the Australian Pharmacy Council (APC) Performance Outcomes domains. The purpose of the reflection is to develop a plan to address these gaps during the course and identify milestones to demonstrate that you have addressed the identified gaps in your knowledge and skills. See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more information and the marking criteria. In the case of a failing mark for this reflection, feedback will be provided and resubmission permitted. This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: L&M Secondary Domain marks: PiP, RIE This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This assessment item will be submitted using ePortfolio, which can be accessed via the PHRM4102 Learn.UQ page
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

assessment_details.title: Professional Skills Exam
assessment_details.due_date: 22/09/2025 This exam will occur during your Stream 2 Tutorial
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is an in-class invigilated exam. A physical AMH book is permitted to be used in the exam. This exam includes demonstration of therapeutic knowledge and management of an integrated case focussing on written communication with other health professionals, and application of legal and ethical responsibilities. The exam will consist of various question types including multiple choice and multiple answer, as well as written short response. See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more information and the marking criteria. The exam will be undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform (as will any deferred exams). Go to the Inspera Assessment Information page link to navigate to the Library Services web page which provides further information on: how to access Inspera, on-campus invigilated exams in Inspera, device requirements, familiarisation opportunities, submitting assessment and access to the Student Laptop Scheme and short-term laptop-loans. Link for Inspera Assessment Information page: Inspera Assessment (Original) - eLearning - University of Queensland This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: PiP, C&C, PEx Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 10 minutes Duration 90 minutes Calculator options (In person) Casio FX82 series only or UQ approved and labelled calculator Open/closed book Closed book examination - specified written materials permitted Materials Physical AMH book Exam platform Inspera Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This exam will be completed using your own device (laptop) requiring an exam-safe browser to access Inspera. Alternatively, you can use one of the desktop computers in the dispensing lab, where the exam will be held.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Late submission
Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .
assessment_details.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty .

assessment_details.title: Poster Presentation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a team assignment with a team mark awarded. Tutorial groups will be split into teams of 4 to 5 students. Each student team will select a real-world problem to which they must apply systems level thinking to develop a systems level solution. The cases will be posted on Learn.UQ (Blackboard) during week 5. Students must develop a poster of their systems level solution to the assigned problem. Each team of students will then present a 5-minute presentation of their case. The expectation is each student will speak during the presentation. Guidance on how to structure the poster and oral presentation, and the marking rubrics for each will be provided on the PHRM4102 Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site. Marks for this item will be adjusted using Peer Assessment of groupwork. This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: RIE Secondary Domain marks: C&C, L&M This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. The poster presentation will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
One team member will submit the team poster via the link provided in the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferral applies only to the in-class presentation. No extensions are available for the poster submission.
Absence on the presentation day must be communicated to the course coordinator and team as soon as possible. The team will present without the absent student and is expected to cover their section/s in some form. The absent student may apply for a deferred presentation which will be completed alone, covering the entire presentation but with a focus on their section/s.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Grand Round Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: Slide deck submission: 20/10/2025 5:00 pm In-class presentation: 21/10/2025
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a team assignment with a team mark awarded. Tutorial groups will be split into teams of 4 to 5 students. Each student team will be assigned an integrated case for which they will outline medication-related problems and their resolution. The assigned cases will be posted on Learn.UQ (Blackboard) during week 9. Each team of students will be expected to present a 10-minute presentation of their case. The expectation is each student will speak during the presentation. Guidance regarding how to structure the presentation and marking rubric will be provided on Learn.UQ (Blackboard). Marks for this item will be adjusted using Peer Assessment of groupwork. You will have the opportunity to ask questions about your cases and the presentation during the tutorials in weeks 10 and 11. This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: PEx Secondary Domain marks: C&C, L&M, RIE The Grand Round Presentation will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

The absent student may apply for a deferred presentation which will be completed alone, covering the entire presentation but with a focus on their section/s.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Oral Viva
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The oral examination will assess knowledge and application to identify medication related problems and discuss therapeutic decision making in choosing options to resolve the medication related problems. This will be an oral VIVA of a patient case during the end of semester examination period. The viva will be 30 minutes in duration including 15 minutes reading and preparation time. During the preparation and reading time only, you will have access to the AMH and eTGs. You will need to bring your own physical hard copy of AMH. After 15 minutes you will commence the oral examination, discussing the medication related problem(s) you have identified and plan to resolve these with the examiner who will then also ask you questions about your case and recommendations. This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: C&C, PEx Secondary Domain marks: PiP This oral viva will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: OSCE
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The PHRM4102 OSCE oral exam will consist of 2 OSCE stations at which students demonstrate a range of skills and knowledge including: Managing a pharmacy presentation: 10-minute station. Students will provide patient-centred care for a consumer presentation. This may take the form of a symptom or product request, or prescription presentation, and will require demonstration of information gathering skills, clinical and scope-of-practice judgements, and counselling skills. Challenging communication: 5-minute station. Students will engage in a challenging verbally communication scenario, potentially involving a patient, customer, or other healthcare provider. The focus of this station is on demonstrating advanced communication skills. Further descriptions, guidance and marking rubrics for these stations will be available on the PHRM4102 Blackboard site. Your own physical AMH book is permitted to be used in the exam The PHRM4102 OSCE stations will be based on concepts, medicine disease states, medicines, scenarios, skills and knowledge you have attained during your pharmacy degree to date. This assessment contributes to the following domains: Primary Domain marks: C&C, PEx Secondary Domain marks: PiP This OSCE oral exam will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 5 To Week 12 (25 Aug - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Stream 1 Tutorials - Advanced Medication Management Attend your timetabled 3 hour Tutorial on Tuesday each week. These tutorials focus on managing complex patient presentations and medication regimens in various clinical settings. Complete the Stream 1 UQ Extend active online learning for the week before coming to the tutorial to test and refresh your knowledge on some relevant practice areas that will be explored. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 5 To Week 13 (25 Aug - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Stream 2 Tutorials - Professional Responsibilities Attend your timetabled 2 hour Tutorial on Monday each week. These tutorials focus on developing and challenging your professional responsibilities, particularly focussing on communication and ethics in various settings. Complete the Stream 2 UQ Extend active online learning for the week before coming to the tutorial to test and refresh your knowledge on some relevant areas that will be explored.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 5 To Week 13 (25 Aug - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Stream 3 Tutorials - Extending Pharmacy Practice Attend your timetabled 2 hour Tutorial on Monday each week. These tutorials focus on expanding the scope of your role as a pharmacist, including thinking in and implementing changes as a system level. Complete the Stream 3 UQ Extend active online learning for the week before coming to the tutorial to test and refresh your knowledge on some relevant areas that will be explored. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L07, L08
Final Lecture The final lecture is a UQ Pharmacy classic event that celebrates the end of your studies within the Pharmacy program. See Learn.UQ for information on this event.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
